questionTextSource: What results in specific risks to motorcyclists?
explanationSource: If you ride with the visor open, an insect can fly into it at any time. This can be very painful and distracting. If the road surface changes, there is a risk of falling down because the grip level is different. In heavy rain, motorbikes are more likely to aquaplane than cars.
answerASource: Riding with the visor open
answerBSource: Riding in heavy rain
answerCSource: Changes in the road surfacing

questionTextSource: What should you do now?
explanationSource: The overtaking vehicle will probably not be able to complete its overtaking manoeuvre as planned. To prevent an accident, you should let the overtaking vehicle get into the lane.
answerASource: I reduce my speed so that the overtaking vehicle can pull in ahead of me
answerBSource: I accelerate so that the overtaking vehicle can pull in behind me
answerCSource: I maintain my speed because there is sufficient safety distance

questionTextSource: Why should you allow the truck to change lane?
explanationSource: If you are in the dead corner of the truck and the truck suddenly exhaust yourself, a dangerous situation can occur and you would have to slow down. Since there is enough space between the vehicle in front, you should take the truck into account and enable it to change lanes.
answerASource: Because the driver of the truck may fail to see me
answerBSource: Because I should show due consideration to the truck
answerCSource: Because the alternate merging procedure applies here

questionTextSource: As a motorcyclist , why is it important that you adopt an anticipatory driving style especially on very winding stretches of road?
explanationSource: By riding with foresight, you can recognise the behaviour of oncoming traffic and potentially dangerous situations, such as dirt on the road or a change in cornering radius, in good time and adapt your riding behaviour accordingly.
answerASource: Because the turning radius can reduce as the road continues
answerBSource: Because otherwise I will not be able to see dirt on the roadway in time
answerCSource: Because it means I am able to adjust myself in good time to the actions of oncoming traffic

questionTextSource: As a motorcyclist, you can easily go unnoticed by other vehicle drivers. How can you reduce this risk?
explanationSource:
Try to put yourself in the shoes of the drivers involved so that you can better understand what they might do next. This way, you may be able to recognise a potential hazard and react to it in good time.
If you are driving in a vehicle's side blind spot, the driver will not be able to see you. If possible, drive in such a way that you are not in the blind spot for an unnecessarily long time. You should also try to make eye contact with the drivers involved before turning to ensure that they can see you.
answerASource: I avoid remaining for too long in the blind spot to the side of other vehicles
answerBSource: I try to see the road situation from the perspective of other vehicle drivers
answerCSource: Before taking a turn, I try to establish eye-contact with other drivers in my vicinity

questionTextSource: What can cause a dangerous situation?
explanationSource: If a foot brake has a strong one-sided effect, the vehicle may swing off laterally during braking. The car may skid and overturn. Therefore, unequal braking forces are extremely dangerous. Excessive steering can also lead to skidding at high speeds because you can no longer keep the lane properly. A disturbed traffic radio is not a technically dangerous defect.
answerASource: Brakes which engage heavily on one side
answerBSource: Too much steering-wheel play
answerCSource: Disturbed reception of traffic information on the radio

questionTextSource: What are the effects of listening to loud music - especially with an extremely loud bass volume - in the car?
explanationSource: Loud music in the car leads to two negative effects: The driver is distracted by the music and he cannot concentrate on the road. Deep basses in particular lead to strong uncertainty in blind road users. These can lose their orientation, especially with low-frequency music.
answerASource: Blind people lose their orientation when they are near the car
answerBSource: The driver’s attention is significantly impaired
answerCSource: It makes it easier for blind people to recognise where the road is

questionTextSource: What can create a hazard when driving?
explanationSource: If the dipped headlights are not working, you will see poorly on the one hand and you will be seen worse on the other. If the service brake is too one-sided, the vehicle could swerve or have a longer braking distance. If there is too much play in the steering, the steering command will not be carried out.
answerASource: A service brake that responds heavily on one side
answerBSource: An excessive amount of play in the steering
answerCSource: A faulty dipped headlight

questionTextSource: You are driving on a narrow road. About 20 m ahead of you a child suddenly runs onto the roadway. When is a collision unavoidable despite emergency braking?
explanationSource: For this question, you must first calculate the thinking distance and the hazard braking distance. At 50 km/h, the thinking distance is 50/10 x 3 = 15. So, there are 15 meters of thinking distance. The hazard braking distance is determined by the following calculation: [(50/10 x 50/10) / 2] = 12.5. 50 km/h is the right answer.
answerASource: 50 km/h
answerBSource: 30 km/h
answerCSource: 20 km/h

questionTextSource: Why do you have to brake?
explanationSource: You have to expect the person standing in front of the stationary truck to move to the left. Braking serves to protect the person.
answerASource: Because someone is walking onto the road
answerBSource: Because passengers are exiting the tram
answerCSource: Because the truck is switching into my lane

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: When turning right, you must always be aware of pedestrians and cyclists approaching. They may cross the road in front of you.
answerASource: I stop
answerBSource: I continue driving as before
answerCSource: I accelerate

questionTextSource: What must you anticipate in this situation?
explanationSource:
The road sign signals increased caution against children who could walk across the road between the vehicles.
In addition, you have to expect that people get off to the side of the road.
answerASource: Children walking out onto the road
answerBSource: People exiting their vehicles via the side nearest the road
answerCSource: There is a pedestrian crossing up ahead

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: Since a person wants to cross the intersection, you have to stop and have the person crossed the traffic light.
answerASource: I stop
answerBSource: I continue driving as before
answerCSource: I accelerate

questionTextSource: What must you reckon with when you are driving in the left-hand lane of an autobahn at high speed?
explanationSource: At high speed, motorcycles are very sensitive to wind from the side. Small fast motorcycles are hard to see, other vehicles might therefore not see you.
answerASource: Motorists in front of you may suddenly pull out to overtake
answerBSource: Even a slight, unexpected side wind can effect road stability
answerCSource: If you have full beam switched on, vehicles travelling in front of you will not pull out to overtake

questionTextSource: When is there a danger of a motorcycle veering sideways at high speed?
explanationSource: Sudden side wind increases the risk of a motorcycle to veer sideways. Sudden side wind can occur when exiting under a bridge, out of a tunnel, or when overtaking a truck.
answerASource: When you overtake a truck
answerBSource: When driving under a bridge at high speed in a strong side wind
answerCSource: When the braking effect is different on both sides of the front disc brake

questionTextSource: What must you reckon with when it starts raining after a longer period of dry weather?
explanationSource: Rain clean up part of the dirt on the road. If the weather was dry for a long period of time, the road will accumulate a lot of dirt. With the addition of water, it will create a film on the road that will make the surface more slippery and increase the braking distance. You can also expect poor visibility on your helmet or goggles.
answerASource: A dangerous greasy film on the surface of the road
answerBSource: Bad visibility through helmet visor or goggles
answerCSource: Shorter braking distance

questionTextSource: You come unexpectedly upon a stretch of road with loose chippings. What do you do?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: Avoid braking if possible
answerBSource: Hold your course if possible
answerCSource: Brake hard using the handbrake

questionTextSource: What can happen if you drive around a left-hand bend near the marked central line?
explanationSource: When turning left, being near the central line is dangerous. Due to the inclination left, you could be hit by a vehicle going in the opposite direction. Road markings such as the central line are slippery, if your motorcycle run on it, it could slip resulting in a fall
answerASource: Because of the inclined position, the upper part of your body could be hit by oncoming traffic
answerBSource: The motorcycle could catch the central line and slip from under you
answerCSource: There is no danger as long as the tyres are new

questionTextSource: What are the dangers on wet cobblestones?
explanationSource: Cobblestones are slippery and will therefore increase the risk of falling or tires to lose adhesion to the road and spin when accelerating as well as a tendency of breaks to lock
answerASource: Increased tendency for the brakes to lock
answerBSource: Increased danger of falling
answerCSource: Spinning and skidding of the rear wheel when accelerating

questionTextSource: What should you particularly remember when it is raining?
explanationSource: Rain reduces visibility. The road surface is less visible when wet. The visibility can also be lowered by water particles coming from other vehicles. Your visor will also accumulate water droplets and further diminish your visibility.
answerASource: Visibility through goggles or helmet visor gets considerably worse
answerBSource: Road surface damage is less noticeable when the road is wet
answerCSource: Visibility is further reduced by water sprayed up by other vehicles

questionTextSource: What should you remember when there are tramlines in your lane?
explanationSource: Tramlines are made of metal which is slippery. A motorcycle therefore has a higher risk of sliding and falling on them. When crossing you should try to be as perpendicular to them as possible to avoid slipping
answerASource: Tramlines should always be crossed at the widest angle possible
answerBSource: There is always greater danger of falling on tramlines
answerCSource: Tramlines should always be crossed at the narrowest angle possible

questionTextSource: What can cause the danger of falling on bends?
explanationSource: When turning on bends, slippery surfaces such as lane markings, oil on the road, dirt, manholes, sand or leaves can lead to a fall
answerASource: Oil on the roadway
answerBSource: Lane markings
answerCSource: The outside edge of the bend is higher than the inside

questionTextSource: What can cause the danger of falling on bends?
explanationSource: When turning on bends or braking, slippery surfaces such as lane markings, oil on the road, dirt, manholes, sand, dust, gravel, wet surfaces, cobblestones or leaves will increase the risk of falling
answerASource: Manhole covers on the roadway
answerBSource: Leaves on the roadway
answerCSource: Sand on the roadway

questionTextSource: When must you apply the brakes with particular care?
explanationSource: Braking is less effective on slippery surfaces (wet road, cobblestones, dirt road) and needs to be applied with care
answerASource: When travelling on cobblestones
answerBSource: When it starts raining
answerCSource: On a concrete roadway with good grip

questionTextSource: What can cause in aquaplaning (sliding on wet roads) on wet road surfaces?
explanationSource: Aquaplaning - also known as hydroplaning - occurs when the tread depth of the tires is too low to displace the accumulated amount of water or when the amount of water to be discharged is too large due to a high speed (from 60 km/h). In addition, wheel ruts in the road, in which water accumulates, favor this effect.
answerASource: High speed
answerBSource: Worn tyres
answerCSource: Grooves in the roadway

questionTextSource: Why could overtaking be dangerous here?
explanationSource: Overtaking in slush can lead to many dangers. Under the snow there could be a layer of ice that makes overtaking dangerous. In addition, you could hurl snow masses at other vehicles or to start swerving back and forth yourself.
answerASource: There is still slush on the overtaking lane
answerBSource: Slush could be thrown up and impair visibility
answerCSource: Because beneath the slush there could also be ice on the road

questionTextSource: What must you do if your vehicle is caught by a strong side wind?
explanationSource: If your vehicle is hit by strong crosswinds, you should not accelerate, slow down instead and countersteer. The faster you drive, the less secure you can keep your vehicle on the road, since crosswinds can lead to skidding of the vehicle.
answerASource: Steer against the wind
answerBSource: Reduce speed
answerCSource: Accelerate rapidly

questionTextSource: When can strong side wind be particularly dangerous?
explanationSource: Changing wind directions are dangerous when driving. Sudden gusts can catch your vehicle and push it aside. You have to countersteer strongly to keep your vehicle in the lane. If the wind changes direction, you have to react quickly and adjust your steering behavior. Strong crosswind is a danger especially on bridges and on level, undeveloped and unwooded terrain. If you overtake one or more trucks, you will have to expect changing pressure conditions when entering the slipstream of the truck and when leaving the truck. At forest aisles or when leaving wooded areas, a strong crosswind can also hit you abruptly.
answerASource: When overtaking trucks
answerBSource: When driving across bridges
answerCSource: When driving past firebreaks in forests

questionTextSource: What must you reckon with here?
explanationSource: If you drive on the motorway in a strong crosswind from the right, the crosswind constantly pushes the car to the left. You have to countersteer to the right to stay in the lane. If you overtake a truck, expect that the pressure from the crosswind is gone immediately as you enter the slipstream of the truck. If you continue to countersteer, there is a risk that you drive against the truck. Since the crosswind comes from the right, the front vehicle could be pushed to the left.
answerASource: Your vehicle could be blown to the left by the strong side wind
answerBSource: When overtaking, your vehicle is pulling to the right when you enter the slipstream of the truck
answerCSource: The vehicle travelling in front of you can be blown to the right

questionTextSource: A strong side wind is blowing from the right. When are you particularly exposed to danger when overtaking?
explanationSource: If there is strong crosswind from the right, you are particularly at risk as a car driver if you enter the slipstream of a truck as part of an overtaking maneuver. This danger arises from the fact that strong side wind from the right pushes your vehicle to the left, whereupon you automatically start countersteering. As you overtake a truck, you fall into its slipstream; the crosswind is instantly gone and you have to adjust the steering. As soon as you get out of the slipstream, the pressure starts again with the crosswind; So, you have to countersteer again.
answerASource: When you enter the slipstream of the truck
answerBSource: When you leave the slipstream of the truck
answerCSource: When you change lane after overtaking

questionTextSource: Why must you reduce your speed in heavy rain?
explanationSource: Water on the windshield or visor breaks the light and worsens the view, whirling up water also obstructs the view. The braking distance is longer on wet roads because of lower grip. The profile of the tires drains the water. At high speed, the amount of water to be drained is too large, there is a risk of aquaplaning. In this case, there is no contact between the road and the tire. The vehicle cannot be controlled any longer.
answerASource: Because braking distance is increased
answerBSource: Because visibility is reduced
answerCSource: Because aquaplaning (sliding on wet roads) can occur

questionTextSource: What adversely affects road safety here?
explanationSource: The rain and the whirled-up water create a haze that can significantly affect traffic safety. Assume that the grip of the tires is significantly lower due to the wet road surface than on dry roads and that this prolongs your braking distance. The low beam of oncoming traffic does not affect the traffic safety, as the car driver is not dazzled by it.
answerASource: Poor visibility
answerBSource: Reduced grip of the tyres on the wet roadway
answerCSource: The dipped headlights of oncoming traffic

questionTextSource: What can be caused if the road surface suddenly becomes worse?
explanationSource: As the road surface deteriorates, it always results in a change and deterioration of traction. As the wheels lose stiction, the vehicle is slipping and skidding faster. The tire noise also depends on the condition of the road surface and changes when, for example, the road turns from a paved road to a cobblestone track.
answerASource: Danger of sliding and skidding
answerBSource: Changes in tyre noise
answerCSource: Longer reaction distance

questionTextSource: What are the special conditions when driving along tree-lined avenues?
explanationSource: The road is usually very narrow. In addition, it is often heavily curved to drain rainwater. Therefore, special care should be taken with oncoming traffic. Curves are not clear due to the trees. You have to drive particularly careful because of the possible oncoming traffic in curves. Left turns are especially dangerous. The trees don't offer any protection to the driver, not even windbreak.
answerASource: Bends are often very narrow and blind
answerBSource: The narrow and often cambered roadway requires special caution when there is oncoming traffic
answerCSource: The trees provide protection from the sides

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: While overtaking a cyclist, the oncoming truck also partly gets on your lane. Brake and drive as far to the right as possible.
answerASource: I brake
answerBSource: I drive over to the right-side as far as possible
answerCSource: I remain in the centre of my lane

questionTextSource: Why should you brake now?
explanationSource: Snow is a special danger for you as a car driver. Your lane is covered in snow. In addition, your view is restricted by a heap of snow. The upcoming green car does not respect the right of way. Therefore, you should brake early, because due to the slipperiness of the braking distance is extended. Probably the green car is also hampered by the snow pile.
answerASource: - green car
answerBSource: - blue car
answerCSource: - tractor

questionTextSource: Why should you ready yourself to have to stop here?
explanationSource: The street looks very narrow. Expect that you have to stop when the truck approaches you. You should drive as far to the right as possible and prepare for a stop to avoid a collision.
answerASource: Because of the truck
answerBSource: Because of the motorcyclist

questionTextSource: What should you be prepared here?
explanationSource: Dirt on the road can increase (not decrease) the braking distance and could make the road more slippery thus affecting the stability of your vehicles
answerASource: - extend the braking distance
answerBSource: - negatively affect driving stability
answerCSource: - reduce the braking distance

questionTextSource: While driving, you unexpectedly come upon a straight section of road with loose chippings. What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: On the straight unexpected section with rolling gravel you should carefully reduce your speed for safety reasons and maintain your driving line as much as possible.
answerASource: I slow down carefully
answerBSource: I keep driving as straight as possible
answerCSource: I use my parking brake to brake sharply

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: Due to the bad weather conditions, you should reduce your speed and avoid jerky steering movements (see danger sign) as you could otherwise skid.
answerASource: I reduce my speed
answerBSource: I avoid abrupt manoeuvres
answerCSource: I steer hard to the right

questionTextSource: What can be the effect of aquaplaning?
explanationSource: In the event of aquaplaning, your vehicle has no direct contact with the road surface, so the steering command and the braking command cannot be implemented.
answerASource: Reduced ability to steer the vehicle
answerBSource: Reduced vehicle braking capability
answerCSource: Increased tyre static friction

questionTextSource: A strong side wind is blowing. When is there a risk that your motorcycle will be shifted sideways at high speed?
explanationSource: Wind conditions change when overtaking a truck, when driving up a bridge and when leaving a tunnel. Be prepared for crosswinds.
answerASource: - overtake a truck
answerBSource: - exit a tunnel passage
answerCSource: - cross a bridge

questionTextSource: What is the correct course of action in this situation?
explanationSource: The traffic sign indicates a risk of skidding and sliding. To avoid skidding, you should reduce your speed, especially when it is raining and leaves are falling on the road, as is the case here. Braking hard, accelerating hard or steering jerkily can cause the tyres to lose their grip and you could skid or slide.
answerASource: I reduce my speed
answerBSource: I avoid abrupt steering movements
answerCSource: I brake hard

questionTextSource: What should you be prepared for here?
explanationSource: Because of the rain, you should be prepared for the fact that other vehicles will not be able to see your vehicle and that your braking distance will be longer due to the reduced grip.
answerASource: For an extended braking distance
answerBSource: For the poor visibility of my vehicle
answerCSource: For increased tyre traction

questionTextSource: Where are you particularly at risk during an overtaking manoeuvre here?
explanationSource: If there is a strong side wind from the right, you as a motorist are particularly at risk if, when driving off-road, you overtake in the slipstream of a truck. This hazard arises from the fact that strong cross winds from the right push your vehicle to the left, whereupon you automatically countersteer. As you overtake a truck, you slipstream into it; the crosswind is gone instantly and you have to adjust the steering. As soon as you pull out of the slipstream, the pressure starts up again with the crosswind; You have to countersteer again.
answerASource: When driving into the truck’s wind-sheltered area
answerBSource: When driving out of the truck’s wind-sheltered area
answerCSource: When changing lane after overtaking

questionTextSource: What could happen if you pass through a bend close to the lane markings?
explanationSource: Always make sure that you and your motorbike are not overhanging the centre line. If you take blind bends, you are unnecessarily endangering yourself and oncoming traffic. This is because you could be hit by oncoming traffic or lose control of your motorbike during evasive manoeuvres.
answerASource: - be struck by oncoming traffic
answerBSource: - lose control of my motorbike while attempting to avoid oncoming traffic
answerCSource: - be able to see well around the bend

questionTextSource: What can increase the risk of a motorcycle fall while driving through road bends?
explanationSource:
Oil on the road increases the risk of falling when cornering, as the tyres' grip is significantly reduced due to the slippery surface.
Unevenness in the road surface, such as bumps, reduces the grip of the tyres and therefore increases the risk of falling.
answerASource: An oil spill on the carriageway
answerBSource: A bump on the carriageway
answerCSource: A carriageway inclines towards the inside of the bend

questionTextSource: How can you reduce the risk of falling when encountering wet road markings?
explanationSource:
As the road markings are slippery, especially when wet, you should avoid driving over them if possible. If it is not possible to drive around them, you should take the shortest route across them to minimise the risk of falling.
Due to the wetness, you should avoid accelerating or braking here to minimise the risk of falling.
answerASource: I avoid driving on the road markings as much as possible
answerBSource: I take the short distance possible when driving across the road markings
answerCSource: I brake as sharply as possible before encountering the road markings

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate when driving on wet cobblestones?
explanationSource:
An uneven and wet road surface and therefore less grip increases the risk of the rear wheel spinning or slipping when accelerating.
When braking on wet cobblestones, the tendency to lock up is increased, which can cause the rear wheel to slip sideways more quickly.
Locking tendency means that the front wheel is loaded more and the rear wheel is unloaded when braking.
answerASource: A high tendency for the wheels to lock during braking
answerBSource: A rapid skidding of the rear wheel when accelerating
answerCSource: An increased traction of the front wheel during steering

questionTextSource: What should you take into account when you drive a motorcycle during low ambient temperatures?
explanationSource:
If the temperature of the outside air differs from the air temperature in the helmet, the helmet will mist up. This greatly impairs visibility. 
In addition, the handbrake lever is more difficult to operate with cold fingers.
answerASource: The helmet visor is prone to misting up
answerBSource: The handbrake lever cannot be safely operated with cold fingers
answerCSource: Freezing improves the ability to concentrate

questionTextSource: What must you anticipate in this situation?
explanationSource:
You can see from the windsock that a strong wind is blowing from the right. The strong wind could move your vehicle to the left. Be prepared to steer to the right.
In addition, when entering the slipstream of the truck, you must be prepared for your vehicle to be moved to the right because its large surface area will keep out the crosswind. Be prepared to steer to the left to avoid colliding with the truck.
answerASource: My vehicle may be forced leftwards
answerBSource: When driving into the wind-protected area alongside the truck, my vehicle may be forced rightwards
answerCSource: The truck ahead of me may be forced rightwards

questionTextSource: Heavy rain has caused large puddles to form on the road surface. What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: You can easily lose control of your motorbike when riding through large puddles. Ride slowly and do not lift your feet to keep your motorbike stable and avoid splashing water.
answerASource: - slowly to avoid create road spray
answerBSource: - quickly to maintain the stability of my motorbike
answerCSource: - with my feet elevated to prevent getting wet

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate while driving, when it starts to rain after a long dry period?
explanationSource: Basically, you must always expect visibility through your helmet visor to be poorer in the rain and, if it is dry for a long time, a layer of dirt will remain on the road which can form a slippery film on the road surface when it rains, so you must expect the road to be particularly slippery.
answerASource: A particularly slippery road surface
answerBSource: Poor visibility through my helmet visor
answerCSource: A shorter braking distance

questionTextSource: Where does aquaplaning (sliding on wet roads) occur particularly often?
explanationSource: If the speed is too high and/or there is more water than can be drained from the tire profiles, aquaplaning occurs. A water film prevents contact between tires and the road. The vehicle is no longer controllable. The risk of aquaplaning exists in particular in ruts and road subsidence. Level crossings usually have no increased risk for aquaplaning, since water can usually run well here.
answerASource: In dips in the roadway
answerBSource: Where there are grooves in the roadway
answerCSource: At level crossings

questionTextSource: What does aquaplaning (sliding on wet roads) lead to?
explanationSource: Aquaplaning or hydroplaning can lead to neither being able to brake or steer. As a result, conscious driving of the vehicle is no longer possible. It can get into a skid. This effect is caused by a kind of water gore, which slides between the tire and the road.
answerASource: The vehicle can no longer be steered and braked
answerBSource: The vehicle can leave the road
answerCSource: Steering becomes heavy

questionTextSource: What must you reckon with when it starts raining after a long period of dry weather?
explanationSource: If rain sets in after prolonged drought, a dangerous film of lubricant forms on the road, which is only washed away after a long period of rain. This film is more dangerous than rain alone. Brake very carefully. This lubricating film is also deposited on the windscreen. After turning on the windscreen wipers, it first worsens the view drastically. The braking distance is extended by the poorer traction.
answerASource: A dangerous greasy film on the surface of the road
answerBSource: Bad visibility immediately upon switching the windscreen wipers on
answerCSource: Shorter braking distance

questionTextSource: It starts raining. Why must you immediately increase the safe distance?
explanationSource: When it starts raining, you must immediately increase the safety distance for the following reasons: Under the influence of moisture, the dirt, dust and fine deposits from tire abrasion or pollen on the road turn into a dangerous film of grease. This reduces the traction and thereby extends the braking distance. This lubricating film also forms on the windscreen and on the brake drums. If you use the windscreen wipers, they will first worsen your view and smudge the window. On the brake drums, this lubricating film leads to a slower response of the brakes in wet conditions.
answerASource: Because a greasy film may form which increases the braking distance
answerBSource: Because visibility may become worse and the windscreen wipers do not immediately provide a clear field of vision
answerCSource: Because the brakes react more quickly

questionTextSource: You are driving in dense fog on a federal road (Bundesstraße) outside a built-up area. Are you allowed to drive in the middle of the road continuously straddling the broken white line?
explanationSource: Driving on the center line is not allowed even in dense fog, especially as drivers can always collide with oncoming traffic due to reduced visibility. If there's fog, slow down and orient yourself on the right side of the road for a safe ride.
answerASource: No, because it could represent a danger to oncoming traffic
answerBSource: Yes, if fog headlamps and rear fog lamps are switched on
answerCSource: Yes, because it reduces the risk of running off the road

questionTextSource: What must you do on encountering sudden dense fog driving at daytime?
explanationSource: As a car driver you must turn on low beam during the day when there's fog, snowfall and other poor visibility. Because of the insufficient luminosity driving with parking light is not permitted. Adjust your speed to the visibility conditions and use the rear fog light when you can see less than 50 meters.
answerASource: Adapt speed immediately to visibility conditions
answerBSource: Switch on dipped headlights
answerCSource: Only switch on parking lights

questionTextSource: How can you achieve sufficient visibility through the windscreen?
explanationSource: Turn your windscreen wipers to interval switching, during fog in order to remove fine water droplets from the mist. Turn on your windscreen wipers before overtaking or being overtaken so that obstructions caused by slush or spray water are immediately removed. Avoid fogged windows with the correct ventilation setting: Set ventilation to outside air supply and not to circulating air. Open the windows a gap to let the moisture escape. Briefly switching on the air conditioning dehumidifies the air very quickly.
answerASource: Switch on the windscreen wipers from time to time when it is foggy
answerBSource: When there is slush, switch on the windscreen wipers already before slush is thrown onto your windscreen by other vehicles
answerCSource: When the windscreen is steamed up, switch the ventilator off and keep the windows closed

questionTextSource: You want to overtake on a narrow avenue not within a built-up area. What must you be aware of in particular?
explanationSource: Due to the narrow width of the lanes, even cyclists cannot be overtaken with oncoming traffic. Often, the road is heavily curved to drain rainwater, that means when crossing the center line, the vehicle tilts to the other side. This load change reduces the driving stability. Due to the trees, curves can be confusing. Vehicles with tall bodies often need to travel in the center to avoid colliding with branches and could reach into your lane.
answerASource: Even cyclists frequently cannot be overtaken when there is oncoming traffic due to the narrow width of the roadway
answerBSource: Misjudgements of distances and oncoming traffic frequently result in serious accidents
answerCSource: Vehicles with high superstructures frequently move to avoid branches

questionTextSource: What do you have to be aware of in this situation?
explanationSource: A sudden and strong change of lighting conditions, you must reduce your speed, because the human eye otherwise cannot react fast enough to the change of light. Other road users or obstacles are very difficult to perceive in dark areas.
answerASource: The speed should be adjusted due to the visibility
answerBSource: Judging distances is made more difficult because of the constant changing of light and shadows
answerCSource: The constant changing of light and shadows makes it more difficult to detect other road users in good time

questionTextSource: When overtaking several trucks, you are faced with this situation. What should you do now?
explanationSource: The visibility is very poor due to the weather and the road is not sufficiently visible. In order not to endanger anyone, you should stop the overtaking process as quickly as possible and ensure that you are at a safe distance from the vehicle in front of you when you go back into lane.
answerASource: I abort the overtaking manoeuvre
answerBSource: After pulling back into lane, I again establish an adequate safety distance
answerCSource: I continue overtaking quickly and pull back into lane in front of the red truck

questionTextSource: You are driving as part of a group of several motorcycles. What reduces the risk of accidents?
explanationSource: When driving together with a group of motorcycles, all should keep their distance behind each other and have enough distance to brake and stop
answerASource: - maintain their position
answerBSource: - assume smaller safety distances
answerCSource: - drive alongside one another in the same lane

questionTextSource: When is there an increased risk of falling during steering and braking manoeuvres?
explanationSource: When turning on bends or braking, slippery surfaces such as lane markings, oil on the road, dirt, manholes, sand, dust, gravel, wet surfaces, cobblestones or leaves will increase the risk of falling
answerASource: On wet cobblestone pavement
answerBSource: On wet asphalt
answerCSource: On carriageways with loose chippings

questionTextSource: Wet road markings increase the risk of a fall. How can you reduce this danger of falling?
explanationSource: Wet markings similarly to tram tracks are slippery. You should cross them as perpendicularly as possible and try to no accelerate or brake on them.
answerASource: Cross road markings at the interstice points
answerBSource: Do not accelerate or brake on markings
answerCSource: Cross long markings at a sharp angle

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: There are two lanes in your direction of travel. You drive on the right side of the two tracks. In oncoming traffic, a snow plow truck approaches. You see in the mirror that a vehicle in the left lane next to you wants to overtake. Snowdrifts hinder you while driving on the lane. You cannot turn left unless the overtaking is completed. Therefore, you must delay further and wait in front of the obstacle until you can dodge to the left.
answerASource: I continue slowing down
answerBSource: I wait before the obstruction
answerCSource: I switch to the left lane now

questionTextSource: What should you do here?
explanationSource: In the event of lane damage before a turn, you should reduce your speed considerably to avoid damaging the vehicle. Due to the confusion of the curve, you should not drive past the lane damage on the left, but continue on the right on your lane.
answerASource: - greatly reduce my speed
answerBSource: - continue driving in my lane
answerCSource: - drive to the left past the damaged road surface

questionTextSource: You are driving on the autobahn. What is the correct thing to do in this situation?
explanationSource: Due to the severely restricted view on the motorway, you can switch on your rear fog light and drive a maximum of 50 km / h.
answerASource: - turn on the rear fog lights
answerBSource: - drive at 50 km/h maximum
answerCSource: - drive at 80 km/h maximum

questionTextSource: The electronic stability programme (ESP) display has flashed several times. What is the correct course of action in this situation?
explanationSource:
The Electronic Stability Program (ESP) is always active. If the ESP light flashes, it shows you that it is actively intervening in the driving behaviour of the vehicle and stabilising it. Therefore, the speed should be reduced.
If the ESP symbol is permanently lit, it is inactive or defective and needs to be checked.
answerASource: I reduce my speed, because it is too high
answerBSource: I maintain my speed, because the ESP ensures I can continue driving safely
answerCSource: I go to a garage, because the ESP display is indicating a fault

questionTextSource: While driving, you use your vehicle’s adaptive cruise control (ACC) system. What must you anticipate in these weather conditions?
explanationSource: The adaptive cruise control system (ASC) independently estimates the distance and speed of the vehicle in front.  This can be limited in bad weather, e.g. due to snow and dirt. Therefore, you must regulate the distance and speed yourself.
answerASource: The functionality of the ACC may be negatively affected
answerBSource: I have to personally control my speed and distance
answerCSource: The ACC stops the vehicle

questionTextSource: You are driving along a narrow avenue. What must you be aware of?
explanationSource: The narrow width of the road and its often curved shape due to tree roots force you to take extra care when cornering and when facing oncoming traffic. Choose your speed in such a way that the vehicle can be brought to a halt within half the visible distance. Example: With a visibility of approx. 40 metres, you should choose your speed so that you come to a stop within approx. 20 metres. In purely mathematical terms, the stopping distance at 30 km/h is 18 metres.
answerASource: - be able to stop within half of the distance visible to me
answerBSource: - stop for oncoming traffic, if necessary
answerCSource: - anticipate unevenness at the edge of the road caused by tree roots

questionTextSource: The electronic stability control (ESC) display light has flashed several times. What is the correct course of action in this situation?
explanationSource: You have to reduce your speed. Due to the slippery road surface and the obviously too high speed in the curves for the road conditions, the wheels lose grip and the car could skid.
answerASource: I reduce my speed, because it is too high
answerBSource: I maintain my speed, because the ESC system ensures I can continue driving safely
answerCSource: I go to a garage, because the ESC display is indicating a fault

questionTextSource: What must you anticipate here?
explanationSource:
The snow on the right and left sides of the road may limit the lane. Therefore, you must expect that the distance to oncoming traffic is reduced.
In addition, you can see from the traffic sign that there is a parking area for walkers on the right-hand side. Therefore, you must expect that there are pedestrians on the roadway.
answerASource: Pedestrians on the carriageway
answerBSource: A reduced amount of side clearance from oncoming traffic
answerCSource: That you may be dazzled by the low-lying sun

questionTextSource: While driving straight ahead, you lose control of your vehicle due to aquaplaning. What is the proper response now?
explanationSource: During aquaplaning, you cannot turn or change the direction of your car. You should also not accelerate or brake. So just carefully take your foot off the gas pedal and try to keep the wheel straight until your wheels touch the road again.
answerASource: I gently take my foot off the accelerator
answerBSource: I keep the steering wheel as straight as possible
answerCSource: I brake firmly

questionTextSource: Why is it dangerous to overtake on this snow-covered carriageway?
explanationSource:
In the event of sneaky, icy road, the tires have less liability and can slip. You should avoid sudden braking through slow, forward -looking driving so as not to skid.
In addition, the in front can also be thrown on the left lane due to the clearing/ stray vehicle in front and your view also deteriorates.
answerASource: Because my vehicle may skid
answerBSource: Because parts of the carriageway may be iced over
answerCSource: Because my vision may become even more negatively affected

questionTextSource: While driving at night on a country road you see pedestrians on your side. What do you do if you are dazzled by an oncoming vehicle?
explanationSource: If an oncoming vehicle dazzles you, be sure to reduce the speed so you can stop at any time in a potentially dangerous situation. If pedestrians are on or near the lane, you may even need to stop in such cases. Increasing your speed is not allowed.
answerASource: Reduce speed and, if necessary, stop
answerBSource: Switch to high beam and proceed without reducing speed
answerCSource: Accelerate in order to get out of the area of dazzle as quickly as possible

questionTextSource: What must you do if wild animals suddenly appear in the full beam of your headlights?
explanationSource: Hold the steering wheel tightly and brake. Dip the high beam, because it dazzles the game and therefore remains on the road. Honk to scare it away. Avoid trying to dodge at high speed. These maneuvers often end at a tree or in oncoming traffic. That's why the following rules apply when there's game on the road: slow down, dip the lights and honk to scare off the game.
answerASource: Immediately dip your headlights
answerBSource: Sound the horn and brake
answerCSource: Proceed on full beam

questionTextSource: What do you have to be aware of in this situation?
explanationSource: Due to the large number of trees and the constant change between light and shade, you have to drive particularly attentively, as it is much more difficult to recognise other road users.
answerASource: - I have to drive with particular care
answerBSource: - I may be poorly visible to other road users
answerCSource: - I am more easily able to judge distances

questionTextSource: In the dark, an oncoming vehicle fails to dip its headlights. What can you do so that you can still see to a certain extent?
explanationSource: If you are dazzled by the light from an oncoming vehicle, look to the right side of the road if possible. This reduces the dazzling effect and you can still stay the lane. It's prohibited to dazzle back by switching on the high beam. Folding down the sun shade is not an appropriate action as it will impair your vision too much.
answerASource: Avoid looking at the headlights of the approaching vehicle and direct your gaze towards the right-hand edge of the roadway
answerBSource: Also switch on full beam
answerCSource: Lower the sun visor

questionTextSource: You are driving on a country road in darkness and rain and encounter oncoming vehicles with dipped headlights. What must you remember?
explanationSource: You must not turn on the high beam in this situation, as this can strongly dazzle oncoming traffic. Especially on wet roads, the reflection of the light is greatly increased. Further interferences include drops of water and streaks on the windscreen, which scatter the light and cloud the view.
answerASource: You can be dazzled by reflections on the wet road
answerBSource: Your wet windscreen can result in your being dazzled
answerCSource: You can avoid possible dazzle by switching on full beam

questionTextSource: What risks could arise if you drive with your main beam lights on?
explanationSource: With high beam on, you can dazzle drivers in front of you and oncoming traffic, which may result in recognizing pedestrians and cyclists not at all or way too late. Game that is crossing the road can also be dazzled, which may lead to the animals remaining in the cone of light. With high beam you can recognize vehicles that broke down better.
answerASource: Oncoming road users will be dazzled
answerBSource: Wild animals on the road will be dazzled and will remain stationary in the light beam
answerCSource: Stationary vehicles on my side of the road will not be detected until it is too late

questionTextSource: Driving at night-time on a rural road, you see persons on your side of the road. What do you do if you are dazzled by an oncoming vehicle?
explanationSource: If you detect people on the side of the road at night as a car driver, you should slow down. Regular eye-contact with the roadside is ideal to avoid an accident. If you are also blinded, you can move to the right and stop.
answerASource: - look towards the right-hand side of the road
answerBSource: - stop if necessary
answerCSource: - turn on my main beam lights

questionTextSource: You are driving on an avenue, with a narrow carriageway. What must you be aware of?
explanationSource: On narrow avenues you are only allowed to drive at a speed that allows you to stop within half the visibility if oncoming traffic approaches. There may be conflicts with oncoming traffic. You may have to slow down, brake and drive to the right or reverse until the road gets wider to allow oncoming traffic drive past you. The roadside of avenues can be particularly uneven because tree roots lift the pavement.
answerASource: I may only drive at a speed that allows me to stop within half of my range of vision
answerBSource: Conflicts can arise with the oncoming traffic
answerCSource: I have to anticipate unevenness at the edge of the carriageway caused by tree roots

questionTextSource: What increases the risk of an accident?
explanationSource:
The risk of accidents is significantly increased if your windscreen and side windows are steamed up or iced over, as you can only see the traffic to a limited extent. Clear all windows before you set off.
You could also be dazzled if you drive towards the low sun. This could make you notice other road users later and increase the risk of an accident. Be extra focussed, wear sunglasses or roll down your sun visor.
answerASource: - with the sun low in the sky out front
answerBSource: - with a misted up windscreen
answerCSource: - with iced over side windows

questionTextSource: You have been driving at high speed for some time. What effects can this have?
explanationSource: You quickly get used to high speeds and you kind of enter a state of a rush. Especially at motorway exits, this can have a fatal impact, as your ability to concentrate and your reaction speed quickly decreases. So, look at the speedometer again and again!
answerASource: You lose your feel for speed
answerBSource: Your powers of concentration diminish
answerCSource: The feel for the right speed on bends grows

questionTextSource: What can result from driving for a long time at high speed?
explanationSource: The senses have become accustomed to the fast pace and you start to underestimate certain situations. Therefore, drivers often drive at high speed into sharp turns on the freeway exit ramps. The fast and monotonous driving decreases the ability to concentrate and thus the readiness to react. Often the safety distance is neglected and drivers drive too close to the car in front.
answerASource: You lose your feel for speed
answerBSource: You drive too close to vehicles in front
answerCSource: Your reactions improve

questionTextSource: When is there a danger of underestimating your own speed?
explanationSource: The danger of underestimating one's own driving speed comes into place when you made a long journey driving at high speed, for example on the highway. Even wide, clear roads quickly beguile you to drive at high speed. On the other hand, a changing roadside development has the opposite effect.
answerASource: When the road is wide and there are no trees, buildings, fences etc. at the edge of the road
answerBSource: When you have already been driving at high speed for some time
answerCSource: When the roadside features (trees, houses, fences) are constantly changing

questionTextSource: What can lead to your dangerously underestimating your own speed?
explanationSource: Wide and clear roads without changing roadside development can lead to dangerous underestimation of one's own speed. But also, longer trips at higher speeds, such as on the highway, can lead to this effect. On the other hand, strong engine and tire noise let the driver perceive the speed more consciously.
answerASource: Driving for a long time at high speed
answerBSource: Driving on a good, wide road
answerCSource: Loud engine and tyre noise

questionTextSource: You are approaching a narrow left-hand bend at high speed. Why must you reduce speed already before entering the bend?
explanationSource: For you as the car driver there is a risk of skidding at increased speed. In the worst case, you could be thrown out of the bend and lose control of the vehicle. The centrifugal forces are too high at an increased speed. The tires can then no longer transfer the braking forces. That's why you should break early enough.
answerASource: - otherwise you will be thrown out of the bend to the right
answerBSource: - you will go into a skid if you do not brake until you are on the bend
answerCSource: - otherwise you will be thrown out of the bend to the left

questionTextSource: What must you do?
explanationSource: In this situation, you should stay below the speed limit. Children or other pedestrians could run onto the roadway between parked vehicles. In addition, you should expect that the doors of the parked vehicles could suddenly be opened to the side of the road.
answerASource: Drive slowly because children might run out from between the parked vehicles at any time
answerBSource: Drive slowly because a door on one of the vehicles could be opened on the roadway side
answerCSource: Drive at the permitted maximum speed because there is no actual apparent danger

questionTextSource: You are approaching a sharp left-hand bend at high speed. Why is it necessary to reduce your speed before you have actually reached the bend?
explanationSource: Due to your high speed, the centrifugal force may cause your vehicle to skid out of the bend on the right. Therefore, you should reduce your speed before the bend.
answerASource: - could slide off to the right along the bend
answerBSource: - could begin to skid
answerCSource: - could slide off to the left along the bend

questionTextSource: In what situation does a driver frequently underestimate their own speed?
explanationSource: When driving constantly at high speed, the ability to concentrate decreases. You get used to the speed. Also, wide roads with little peripheral development make you less aware of how fast you are actually going. The reaction time is prolonged.
answerASource: On wide, well-finished roads
answerBSource: After a long journey at high speed
answerCSource: On narrow roads with uneven road surfaces

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate when driving at high speed on the motorway?
explanationSource:
Other road users may underestimate your speed and suddenly pull out to overtake, for example. You may not be able to brake in time at high speed if a dangerous situation arises. If you see that danger is imminent, reduce your speed accordingly beforehand. 
In addition, at high speeds, even a slight crosswind can cause your vehicle to veer to the right or left.
answerASource: Other road users underestimating my speed
answerBSource: Cross winds can negatively affect my stability on the road
answerCSource: I might not respond to hazards in good enough time

questionTextSource: What is especially important when choosing the speed for driving along tree-lined avenues with narrow roadways?
explanationSource: When driving on an avenue with narrow lane width, you must consider the following factors when choosing your speed: Due to the rows of trees on the side, the lighting conditions can change quickly. This makes it difficult for you to estimate the distances to the sides and oncoming traffic. You have to choose your speed so that you can stop halfway on the track, which you can overlook. This is particularly important because wide or tall vehicles often drive in the middle of avenues due to space requirements.
answerASource: To estimate the lateral distance to the trees and oncoming traffic simultaneously
answerBSource: It may be necessary to stop within half the visible distance when a wide vehicle is coming from the opposite direction
answerCSource: Rapidly changing light conditions make it easier to estimate lateral distances

questionTextSource: Why are you required to slow down now?
explanationSource: An animal runs on the road from the right. Since there is no vehicle behind you, you should delay, brake and drive to the right. This is the safest way to handle possible scenarios: the tractor could hit the animal and start skidding, the tractor could drive on your lane in an evasive maneuver, you could hit the animal and lose control of your vehicle.
answerASource: Because of the animal
answerBSource: Because of the tractor
answerCSource: Because of the course of the road

questionTextSource: Where must you anticipate dangerous situations suddenly arising?
explanationSource: You must be aware of sudden, dangerous situations when: 1. visibility is severely restricted; 2. Persons or animals could unexpectedly appear on the road. In the case of limited visibility, you may detect obstacles such as broken vehicles on the roadway too late. You have less time to react accordingly. In forest areas you have to expect wild animals crossing suddenly during dusk times; in residential areas you have to expect children playing on the street. Caution is also required in front of schools and at school bus stops.
answerASource: In forested areas
answerBSource: After hilltops
answerCSource: In front of schools

questionTextSource: Why do you have to lower your speed here?
explanationSource: The vehicle overtaking in front of you dangerously carries an accident risk, as it can lead to a collision. In order to prevent participation and also to give the other vehicles space to evade, your own speed must be reduced.
answerASource: Because of the vehicle overtaking
answerBSource: Because of the speed limit
answerCSource: Because of the low volume of traffic

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate in this situation?
explanationSource: With a confusing right turn you have to expect different situations. Especially at dusk times, wild animals can switch sides right after the turn. But it can also happen that oncoming traffic cuts the curve and is recognized very late or that a vehicle is driving at a very low speed right after the curve.
answerASource: Slow-moving vehicle on my side of the road
answerBSource: Fast oncoming traffic cutting the corner
answerCSource: Wild animals crossing the road

questionTextSource: Why do you have to reduce your speed and keep as far to the right as possible here?
explanationSource: In this situation, because of the yellow car coming in the opposite direction, you have to reduce your speed and keep as far to the right as possible
answerASource: Because of the [red car]
answerBSource: Because of the speed limit
answerCSource: Because of the [blue truck]
